title: Fitzpatrick Fires 63 to Lead RBC Heritage at Harbour Town
excerpt: Matt Fitzpatrick's brilliant second round of 8-under 63 puts him atop the leaderboard at the RBC Heritage Signature Event, one shot ahead of Viktor Hovland.
body:
Fitzpatrick Takes Command at Harbour Town

Matt Fitzpatrick fired an 8-under 63 in the second round to seize the lead at the 2026 RBC Heritage, one of the PGA Tour's most prestigious post-Masters events. Playing at the iconic Harbour Town Golf Links in Hilton Head, South Carolina, the 30-year-old Englishman has positioned himself perfectly heading into the weekend.

Fitzpatrick's round was a masterclass in precision shot-making, capped by what he called "one good break and a lot of great golf." He holds a one-shot advantage over Viktor Hovland, who remains firmly in contention after a strong start to the week at Harbour Town, the par-70 course that demands accuracy over length.

Fitzpatrick's R2 Score
8-under 63
Tournament Leader
Fitzpatrick
Course Par
70
Signature Event Prize Pool
$20m

Hovland in Close Pursuit

Viktor Hovland, the world's top-ranked player and a two-time RBC Heritage champion, is just one stroke back and represents the primary challenge to Fitzpatrick's coronation. Hovland's consistency at Harbour Town — a course that rewards the methodical, European-style approach both men employ — makes him a formidable test over 36 holes of weekend golf.

The pairing of Fitzpatrick and Hovland in Sunday's final group will be must-watch television for any serious golf fan, two of the game's finest ball-strikers squaring off on a course that punishes wayward tee shots and demands precision around the greens.

Scheffler, Young, and the Depth Field

Scottie Scheffler, the world No. 1 and defending Masters champion, is well-positioned within striking distance, while Cameron Young — who won The Players Championship just two weeks ago — remains in contention to land back-to-back Signature Events. The field at Harbour Town is stacked with recent form, post-major adrenaline, and players hungry for silverware.

Looking Ahead

With two rounds complete, 36 holes remain. Harbour Town's tight fairways and lightning-quick greens have historically produced drama in the final rounds. Fitzpatrick will need to hold his nerve; Hovland, the favourite, will be plotting his move. It's shaping up to be a weekend worthy of the event's prestige.
